feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Modern Life May Be Pushing Human Minds Beyond Their Limits, Reflecting an Evolutionary Mismatch
entry.summary: Try to think back to the last day you remember that wasn't packed full of things to do, people to see, and places to go.

entry.title: Higher consumption of sweeteners linked to decline in memory
entry.summary: Researchers discovered adults who consumed heavier amounts of sweeteners showed a 62% faster decline.

entry.title: Doctor warns these 7 everyday habits can shorten your health span after 50, most people ignore them
entry.summary: Turning 50 often changes the way people look at life. Children may have grown up, careers may be more stable, and many finally find time for themselves. Yet, while life may seem to slow down, the body quietly enters one of its most important phases.This is th…

entry.title: Malaria Vaccine's Achilles' Heel: Its 4th Dose
entry.summary: Getting parents in Africa to bring their kids back in for that final booster shot has been a challenge

entry.title: How Flossing Impacts More Than Just Your Oral Health—What to Know
entry.summary: Dental experts share the effects of flossing on your overall health—and why you should be practicing this habit daily.

entry.title: NC farmers see increased produce sales as cyclosporiasis outbreak continues
entry.summary: Shoppers are turning to locally grown produce sold at the State Farmer’s Market as health officials investigate the cyclosporiasis outbreak in North Carolina to find a source.

entry.title: An avocado a day keeps the doctor away: new study
entry.summary: New research has found the brunch menu staple is associated with a major health benefit for the heart.

entry.title: Tracking Cyclosporiasis: 30 more in Wake diagnosed with diarrhea-causing bug
entry.summary: More than 30 people got a Cyclosporiasis diagnosis on Thursday in Wake County, pushing the total number of people dealing with the diarrhea-causing bug to 270.

entry.title: You don't have to drink alcohol to damage your liver: Doctor reveals 9 unexpected factors that could be putting it at risk
entry.summary: Most people think about the liver only when alcohol enters the conversation. But the truth is far more surprising. The liver is one of the hardest-working organs in the body. Every day, it filters blood, breaks down medicines, stores nutrients, regulates chol…

entry.title: Sweeteners may slow down growth of gut bacteria
entry.summary: Researchers found that two very important gut bacteria were directly impacted by one particular sweetener.

entry.title: Heavy TV watching associated with smaller brain structures, study finds
entry.summary: "Turn off that TV, it'll rot your brain!" has been a household refrain for decades. While "rot" might be too strong a term, researchers are finding that the overall sentiment could have some merit.

entry.title: Stomach bugs surging as doctors say certain travel essentials could save your trip
entry.summary: With travelers' diarrhea the most common illness during summer travel, the CDC says packing oral rehydration salts and loperamide can help manage symptoms.

entry.title: Second person dies of Legionnaires’ disease as New York City cases decline sharply
entry.summary: A second person has died of Legionnaires’ disease in connection with an outbreak on New York City’s Upper East Side that has sickened 72 people and prompted cleanup efforts at the world-famous Metropolitan Museum of Art and Guggenheim Museum.

entry.title: The Best After-Lunch Drink for Better Digestion
entry.summary: If you’re looking to support your digestion after lunch, a warm cup of ginger tea may be the best option. Here’s what dietitians have to say.

entry.title: Is intelligence related to happiness?
entry.summary: We often imagine the brooding genius burdened by intellect, or assume that ignorance is bliss. But what does the data say? Decades of psychological research reveal a highly complex relationship between cognitive ability, emotional skills, and human joy.

entry.title: Can Intermittent Fasting Help Reduce Visceral Fat?
entry.summary: Some dietary changes and patterns, like intermittent fasting, may be effective for shrinking visceral fat, which could protect your health.

entry.title: My Husband Magically Developed a Rogue STI. He Swears He Didn’t Cheat, but, Uh, What the Hell?
entry.summary: I want to believe his doctor, but I'm not convinced.

entry.title: The ‘ice cream paradox’ — how the treat can actually be good for your health
entry.summary: With Sunday marking National Ice Cream Day, experts are giving the scoop on how the sweet summer treat can actually be good for your health.

entry.title: 24 GLP-1 Users Reveal The Good, The Bad, And The Shocking Side Effects
entry.summary: "I’m the one that has to live in my body, I’m doing what I need to get my life back, and that’s all that matters."

entry.title: Potato Salad vs. Pasta Salad: Which Is Better for Blood Sugar?
entry.summary: If you’re watching your blood sugar, dietitians say you don’t have to choose between potato salad and pasta salad. Here’s why.

entry.title: This Medication Is Everywhere—And It May Slow Biological Aging
entry.summary: New research suggests that GLP-1 medications may slow the aging process. Here's why.

entry.title: Robot doesn't perform the surgery: Cardiac surgeon debunks the biggest myths around robotic heart surgery
entry.summary: Health News: \"Knowledge is power,\" said philosopher Francis Bacon, and nowhere is that more relevant than when it comes to heart surgery. Hearing the words robotic.

entry.title: Simple Ways to Prevent Age-Related Muscle Loss
entry.summary: A video outlines practical ways to slow age-related muscle decline and why accessible routines matter for daily function. It details how strength training tw...

entry.title: I Have a Genetic Risk for Alzheimer’s. I Made These 5 Lifestyle Changes to Preserve My Brain Health
entry.summary: After her father’s Alzheimer’s diagnosis, one woman learned she had a rare genetic risk for the disease. She made major lifestyle changes to maintain brain health.

entry.title: Breast Cancer Survivor Shares What Her Hair Looks Like After Chemotherapy
entry.summary: A UK-based breast cancer survivor took to Instagram to share how her hair grew back after chemotherapy and her journey towards accepting that it looked different from how it did before the treatment

entry.title: Creatine supplements may help halt cancer growth, study suggests
entry.summary: A new study has found that creatine can provide a crucial energy boost to key immune cells, allowing them to effectively attack and slow the growth of cancer tumors.

entry.title: Eliminating These 3 Foods From Your Diet Can Be A Game-Changer For Heart Health
entry.summary: Straight from the mouths of cardiologists and a heart health nutritionist.

entry.title: Scientists Think Eating Potatoes May Have Changed Human Digestion Over Thousands of Years
entry.summary: Potatoes have long been a cornerstone of the Andean diet. Now, new research suggests that history may also be reflected in the genes of Indigenous Peruvian Andean populations, who carry the highest known number of copies of the AMY1 gene, which helps the body…

entry.title: Here's What It Means If You Get Winded From Walking Up The Stairs
entry.summary: Most people have been out of breath after climbing the stairs — but is that normal?

entry.title: Here’s What Longevity Experts Eat For Breakfast To Add Years To Their Lives
entry.summary: If you’re going to have the same meal on repeat every morning, how great would it be if it could give you more time on Earth?

entry.title: 10 Everyday Habits That Are Harming Your Longevity The Most
entry.summary: Doctors share what you shouldn't be doing if you want to live a long and healthy life.

entry.title: My mom's long-term care has cost almost $400,000. This is after we spent $45,000 converting a guest house on our property for her.
entry.summary: Alison George's mom has Alzheimer's and her long-term living facilities have cost almost $400,000. They also spent $45,000 to convert a guest house.

entry.title: The Best Bread to Eat if You Have High Blood Pressure, According to Dietitians
entry.summary: If you’re looking for a more blood pressure-friendly loaf, dietitians say that sprouted whole-grain bread is a great pick.

entry.title: Pa. Lyme disease cases near historic highs in Allegheny, Westmoreland counties
entry.summary: With more than 1,300 cases of Lyme disease already reported in Allegheny and Westmoreland counties this year, Western Pennsylvania remains one of the most heavily impacted regions in the nation for the tick-borne illness. According to preliminary 2026 data fr…

entry.title: Coffee may help the body fight stress and aging through a hidden cellular switch
entry.summary: Scientists may have uncovered one reason coffee is repeatedly associated with healthier aging and lower disease risk. Compounds in coffee appear to activate NR4A1, a receptor that helps protect cells from stress, inflammation, and damage. These protective eff…

entry.title: Visit a museum to help slow aging, nap between 1 and 3 p.m. and uncross your legs — plus 6 more health tips to help you have a great week
entry.summary: Health hacks for the week ahead — including how adding some spice to your meals can improve heart health.

entry.title: A personal trainer says these are the four exercises every walker should be doing to build walking strength
entry.summary: Get your muscles ready for longer distances with simple movements that don’t require any special equipment

entry.title: Second person dies in Manhattan Legionnaires' cluster
entry.summary: The city Health Department announced the second death late Saturday night.

entry.title: Utah podcaster diagnosed with cancer aims to give back after 'overwhelming' community support
entry.summary: A popular Utah podcaster who was recently diagnosed with cancer is looking to give back after receiving overwhelming financial support for his treatment.

entry.title: Feline parvovirus outbreak reported in Henrico neighborhood
entry.summary: Officers said several other cats may have been exposed to the virus.

entry.title: Henrico warns residents of feline parvo virus outbreak
entry.summary: Henrico County Police’s Animal Protection Unit is warning residents of a confirmed Feline Panleukopenia “Parvo” virus outbreak in the area.

entry.title: What Happens to Your Sleep When You Eat a High-Protein Snack Before Bed
entry.summary: A high-protein snack before bed may ease hunger, but timing matters. Learn when to eat one and when it could disrupt sleep.

entry.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
entry.summary: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?

entry.title: Health officials demand access to Aurora ICE facility over tuberculosis case
entry.summary: ICE did not directly answer whether public health officials have been allowed inside, State and county health leaders also did not respond to FOX31's questions on Saturday.

entry.title: Leading oncologist reveals 6 habits that could promote longevity and reduce your risk of chronic illness
entry.summary: Dr. Ezekiel Emanuel says dinner parties combine healthy food, mental activity, social life and exercise into one simple longevity strategy.

entry.title: 'Great news': Measles spread slows in Utah
entry.summary: A statewide measles outbreak that affected over 100 Utahns seems to be coming to an end with just six infections in the last three weeks.

entry.title: New Alzheimer's blood test predicts who is likely to develop dementia in 5 to 10 years
entry.summary: A p-tau217 blood test predicted a 78% chance of cognitive decline within 10 years for symptom-free adults with very high levels of the biomarker.

entry.title: Too Much Happiness Sent A Woman To The Emergency Room In an Incredibly Rare Case
entry.summary: The phrase "I could die of happiness" is not meant to be taken literally.

entry.title: Cyclospora cases prompt questions about produce safety; Utah cases not linked to national outbreak
entry.summary: A growing Cyclospora outbreak across parts of the country is causing concern among consumers and raising questions about whether fresh produce is safe to eat.

entry.title: Florida bloom toxins turn up in air, suggesting inhalation risk beyond shorelines
entry.summary: Research on Southwest Florida cyanobacterial blooms shows that the toxins they produce are aerosolized and potentially inhaled by people far from contaminated water bodies.
